body_txt: A woman gives birth to a live baby minutes, hours, or even days after her own death. Depending on the child, they may be seen as a miracle, Born Unlucky , cursed, or anywhere in between. Also counts for cases where the mother was undead at the time of the birth, which can result in an Undead Child . Cases where the mother is brain-dead but kept alive by life support can also count. In real life, a child can be born from a dead woman but if the woman is not on life support, there are only a few minutes before the child suffocates without the oxygen provided by the mother's blood flow. The longer before the child is extracted, the likelier it is that oxygen deprivation leads to brain damage. The fetus also has to be sufficiently far developed to not die as soon as the blood stops flowing. Accounts of dead women giving birth go back centuries and referred to as coffin births, the general term being post-mortem fetal extrusion. Some historical cases will have come from the woman being Buried Alive in a time it was more difficult to determine whether someone was really dead. Other cases, if not most, were not that gruesome. In those cases, the mother and child both died, but, in a process still not fully understood, the gasses generated by decomposition expelled the fetus post-mortem. In neither scenario was the child truly born from a dead woman but anyone exhuming the graves might have been given that impression. Compare Death by Childbirth , in which the mother usually dies after the delivery. A Traumatic C-Section may or may not be fatal to the mother, but prior to modern medicine, it was usually performed when the mother had already died in an attempt to save the baby. A subtrope of Missing Mom , as well as Birth-Death Juxtaposition . May occur as a result of Imperiled in Pregnancy . Contrast with Tragic Stillbirth and Convenient Miscarriage , where it's the child who dies prior to the birth and the mother (usually) lives. As a Death Trope all spoilers will be unmarked .
